name: Beef Liver
summary: One of the most nutrient-dense foods on earth — a small serving delivers many times a day's vitamin B12 and preformed vitamin A, plus highly-absorbable iron.
superfoodReason: Gram for gram among the most nutrient-dense foods available, concentrating vitamin B12, preformed vitamin A, iron, copper, and folate.

benefits.claim: Exceptional vitamin B12 and iron for blood and hair
benefits.mechanism: Vitamin B12 is required to make red blood cells and myelin and to run energy metabolism; deficiency causes fatigue and anemia. Liver is one of the densest B12 sources, and the heme iron alongside it is far better absorbed than plant iron. A 2019 review of vitamins and minerals in hair loss identifies both iron and B12 deficiency as recognised, reversible contributors to hair shedding, so nutrient-dense liver supports hair too.

citations.title: Vitamin B12 — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)
The Role of Vitamins and Minerals in Hair Loss: A Review (Almohanna et al., Dermatol Ther 2019)

benefits.mechanism: Liver supplies vitamin A as retinol — the ready-to-use form — supporting vision, immunity, and skin. It's so concentrated that a little goes a long way (and pregnant people are advised not to overdo it).

citations.title: Vitamin A and Carotenoids — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Liver is so dense in preformed vitamin A that polar-explorer accounts describe vitamin A poisoning from eating polar-bear or seal liver — a reminder that with liver, more is not better.
